"Title: Cognitive Correlates of Spatial Reasoning in Urban Youth. Abstract: This study investigated the relationship between spatial reasoning abilities and neighborhood characteristics in a sample of 200 adolescents from diverse socioeconomic backgrounds. Participants completed a standardized spatial reasoning test and provided demographic information, including postal code. Results indicated a significant positive correlation between spatial reasoning scores and the percentage of owner-occupied housing in participants' neighborhoods. Furthermore, qualitative analysis of interview data revealed that adolescents from neighborhoods with higher rates of single-parent households reported greater difficulty visualizing three-dimensional objects. These findings suggest that environmental factors play a crucial role in the development of spatial cognition."

Results indicated a significant positive correlation between spatial reasoning scores and the percentage of owner-occupied housing in participants' neighborhoods. Furthermore, qualitative analysis of interview data revealed that adolescents from neighborhoods with higher rates of single-parent households reported greater difficulty visualizing three-dimensional objects. These findings suggest that environmental factors play a crucial role in the development of spatial cognition."igma, im unsure how representative that extract presents data on such delicate populations
